F1A authorization package authored 2026-07-24 (ORCHESTRATOR -> SPEC_AUTHOR -> WORK_ORDER_AUTHOR, Claude, provider-neutral role contract): ADR-OW-005 (docs/decisions/ADR_2026-07-24_F1A_VERSIONED_CONTRACT_FOUNDATION.md) decides JSON Schema draft 2020-12, a URN $id/namespace convention, SemVer 1.0.0 initial versions with immutable-release/no-silent-replacement compatibility rules, closed-world semantics with exactly two named/bounded opaque fields (operational-session.metadata, command/event-envelope.payload), shared identifier/semver/timestamp/riskClass definitions, cross-contract identity invariants including new correlation_id/causation_id/sequence fields on event-envelope and a new expected_version field on command-envelope (both absent from the operations-workspace-all-phases design input), provider-neutrality, the exact five-schema F1A set (profile-manifest, operational-session, command-envelope, event-envelope, capability-manifest -- capability-invocation explicitly excluded), a standalone common-definitions.schema.json shared-definitions file (six files total), offline/deterministic $ref resolution via a local schema registry, and a pinned-jsonschema validator strategy (new root file requirements-contract-validator.txt, exact version+hash resolved and disclosed at BUILD time, never an unpinned/network-resolved install) resolving the confirmed gap that this repository has no pyproject/package manifest and jsonschema is not installed. OW-F1A-SPEC-001 (docs/specs/F1A_VERSIONED_CONTRACT_FOUNDATION_SPEC.md) sets 29 acceptance criteria (F1A-AC-01 through F1A-AC-29). OW-F1A-WO-001 (docs/work_orders/F1A_VERSIONED_CONTRACT_FOUNDATION_WORK_ORDER.md) sets the exact BUILD changed-set ceiling (six schema files, one validator dependency manifest, one test file, one BUILD evidence file, a conditional Module Registry/Catalog change, and continuity paths), a separate Codex-only C3 closure ceiling, and a four-commit plan (C1 authorization, C2 BUILD, C3 independent review receipt, C4 post-push closure synchronization only). This round did not touch the roadmap, docs/catalog/**, docs/INDEX.md, contracts/**, tests/**, scripts/**, .cvf/**, AGENTS.md, or either read-only input. No BUILD occurred. Claude does not self-grant REVIEW_PASS and did not stage, commit, or push any file. Next governed move: Codex acts as independent REVIEWER over ADR-OW-005, OW-F1A-SPEC-001, and OW-F1A-WO-001.

F1A authorization repair round 1 (2026-07-24, REPAIR_WORKER, Claude, provider-neutral role contract): independent Codex review returned F1A_AUTHORIZATION_REVIEW_FAIL with five findings, all repaired without waiver. F1A-R1 INCOMPLETE_HASH_LOCK_STRATEGY -- withdrew the false claim that requirements-contract-validator.txt would hold one package with no further dependency; live-resolved and hash-verified the real 19-package transitive closure (jsonschema[format-nongpl]==4.26.0 plus 18 dependencies, every hash computed directly from a downloaded wheel via hashlib.sha256, not copied from an unverified source), authorized exactly one supported interpreter/platform (CPython 3.13 / win_amd64, matching this repository's verified environment, because rpds-py's wheel is platform/interpreter-specific and is jsonschema's own hard dependency), and required pip install --require-hashes into a temporary isolated virtual environment (never the user/global Python), never --no-deps, with BUILD stopping on dependency-resolution drift, a missing compatible wheel, an unpinned/unhashed package, or a source-build fallback. A real resolver pitfall was found and disclosed in the same round: a single pip install --dry-run --report command silently omitted two genuine runtime dependencies (idna, six) because it resolves against the currently-running environment's already-satisfied packages rather than a clean target; the corrected method (a fresh --target install plus per-package pip download --no-deps and direct hashing) is now the mandated BUILD discipline. F1A-R2 SEMVER_CONTRACT_CONTRADICTION -- replaced the mislabeled 'full SemVer 2.0.0' grammar with a narrower stable-release MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH pattern (no prerelease/build-metadata suffix, no leading zeroes except zero itself) applied consistently in both the version-policy decision and the shared semver definition, with explicit accept/reject test values. F1A-R3 TIMESTAMP_FORMAT_ASSERTION_GAP -- withdrew the false claim that the timestamp regex alone guarantees RFC3339 validity; confirmed live this round that bare jsonschema does not even register a date-time format checker without the format-nongpl extra, and that the regex alone accepts calendar-impossible strings (month 13, day 40, hour 25, Feb 30); corrected policy requires jsonschema.FormatChecker on every instance-validation path, with the regex restated as defense-in-depth only, plus new negative tests for calendrically-impossible-but-structurally-matching timestamps and a test proving FormatChecker is actually supplied. F1A-R4 OPAQUE_BOUNDARY_CLAIM_UNENFORCED -- withdrew the unenforceable claim that metadata/payload 'cannot carry any contract-owned field name'; adopted the bounded rule that opaque nested keys may share a name with a top-level field but never override or bypass that top-level field's own validation, with three new required tests (arbitrary nested keys accepted; the same unknown key at the envelope top level rejected; a nested state/version key does not satisfy the required top-level state/version field). F1A-R5 NONDETERMINISTIC_MODULE_REGISTRY_ENTRY -- replaced 'something like' language in the work order and spec with the exact authorized Module Registry entry, verbatim, no discretion left to BUILD. Repaired exactly the six paths this round's ceiling permits (ADR-OW-005, OW-F1A-SPEC-001, OW-F1A-WO-001, IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.json, CVF_SESSION/ACTIVE_SESSION_STATE.json, CVF_SESSION/handoffs/AGENT_HANDOFF_V1_2026-07-23.md); no seventh path; no contracts/**, tests/**, requirements file, catalog, Index, roadmap, source, script, core-pin, or runtime path touched; no BUILD occurred; no secret was read; the package-registry (PyPI) reads performed to resolve/hash the validator dependency closure required no credentials and are not an AI/agent-provider call. Claude does not self-grant REVIEW_PASS and did not stage, commit, or push any file. Status: REPAIRED_PENDING_INDEPENDENT_RE_REVIEW. Next governed move: Codex acts as independent REVIEWER performing re-review of ADR-OW-005, OW-F1A-SPEC-001, and OW-F1A-WO-001 as repaired.

No other content changed -- the package table, versions, hashes, supported platform (CPython 3.13 / win_amd64), dependency policy, install policy, SemVer policy, FormatChecker policy, opaque-boundary policy, Module Registry entry, acceptance criteria, and BUILD ceiling are all unchanged; OW-F1A-SPEC-001 and OW-F1A-WO-001 needed no edits (neither ever repeated the '17 of 19' figure). F1A-R1 through F1A-R5 and their repair round 1 history remain closed and unaltered. Repaired exactly this ADR plus the three continuity/status files, still inside the same six-path total ceiling; no seventh path; no contracts/**, tests/**, requirements file, catalog, Index, roadmap, source, script, core-pin, or runtime path touched; no BUILD occurred; no secret was read; no provider call was made. Claude does not self-grant REVIEW_PASS and did not stage, commit, or push any file. Status: REPAIRED_PENDING_INDEPENDENT_RE_REVIEW_2. XR1 authorization repair round 1 (2026-07-24, REPAIR_WORKER, Claude, provider-neutral role contract): independent Codex review returned seven findings, all repaired without waiver. XR1-R1 STALE_POST_F1A_BASELINE -- all three authorization artifacts were still stated against the pre-F1A-BUILD baseline and called F1A BUILD uncommitted/awaiting review; corrected throughout to the current Operations HEAD/origin 0efa7f23bfb2ea5677b680ed35ca0ae3f057e715, F1A CLOSED/FROZEN/PARKED (C1 d731762a9e135b075261831ed7eb0df4badc98dd, C2 9e59cfdcf3d1da2644540088e748123cd41f14e9, C3 4cdd7f06e040fee43ab733d3dc608aa4d425452b, C4 0efa7f23bfb2ea5677b680ed35ca0ae3f057e715), and the 177-test regression baseline; F1A itself was not reopened. XR1-O-C1's wording was also corrected to name exactly the six authorization paths, removing a redundant 'plus authorization continuity' phrase. XR1-R2 ACCOUNTING_MODEL_AMBIGUITY -- defined one unambiguous comparison universe (base tree B, candidate tree C, matched-rename-pair set R) and one counting unit (a rename is one logical record carrying oldPath+newPath, never two entries), with the exact equation unchanged+modified+added+deleted+renamed = |B union C| minus |R|, plus new required tests for additions, deletions, a pure rename, and a rename-with-content-change. XR1-R3 UNVERIFIABLE_APPLY_AUTHORIZATION -- defined a versioned apply-manifest schema (new scripts/linked_sources/apply_manifest.schema.json, added to the XR1-O-C2 BUILD ceiling) binding schemaVersion, workspaceId, baseCommit, candidateCommit, scan dataset SHA-256, filtering-policy SHA-256/version, per-entry source path/blob hash, destination path, operation, an authorization receipt path, the exact authorization commit, and the manifest's own SHA-256; apply must verify that manifest and receipt blobs exist at that exact commit, that the commit is reachable from Operations origin/main, and that the receipt records REVIEW_PASS for that exact manifest hash -- a scan report, a tracked draft, an uncommitted file, or a self-declared status field is never sufficient. XR1-R4 DESTINATION_PATH_ESCAPE -- added fail-closed validation and required negative tests for absolute/drive/UNC paths, empty/dot/parent-traversal components including mixed separators, NUL/ADS/colon/reserved names, case-insensitive and Unicode-normalization collisions, duplicate destinations, .git and protected governance/continuity/catalog destinations, symlink/junction/reparse-point ancestors, repository-root escape, and Git modes 120000 (symlink) / 160000 (submodule) which are never applied and remain quarantine-only. XR1-R5 PARTIAL_APPLY_AND_ROLLBACK_GAP -- required the rollback/preimage bundle to be fully written and hash-verified before the first destination mutation, atomic per-file replacement via temp-file-plus-rename, full restoration of every previously-mutated path plus a failure/recovery receipt on any mid-batch failure, an explicit disclaimer that cross-filesystem atomicity is not claimed (only all-or-restore behavior is), and a new test injecting failure after the first write to prove zero residual content delta. XR1-R6 UNSAFE_DISCOVERY_OR_CLONE -- replaced open-ended sibling discovery with a bounded three-step resolution order (validated ignored local binding; exact expected sibling directory whose origin matches; explicit --clone-to or a documented deterministic default), forbidding any filesystem search and any overwrite/reuse of a non-empty or wrong-remote directory, and clarifying that local-workspace-link.json is ignored local execution state, may be created during a run, and is never staged or committed. XR1-R7 FILTER_PRECEDENCE_AND_SECRET_CONTENT -- defined the deterministic precedence HARD_EXCLUDE > PROTECTED_SOURCE_ONLY > QUARANTINE_REVIEW > ELIGIBLE_CANDIDATE, restated APPROVED_APPLY as an apply/review lifecycle state the scan engine can never emit, and added secret-content detection requirements using only safe synthetic fixtures, with reports recording exclusively classification/reason/hash metadata and never persisting secret bytes or matched values. Repaired exactly the six paths this round's ceiling permits (ADR-OW-006, OW-XR1-SPEC-001, OW-XR1-WO-001, IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.json, CVF_SESSION/ACTIVE_SESSION_STATE.json, CVF_SESSION/handoffs/AGENT_HANDOFF_V1_2026-07-23.md); no seventh path; no BUILD, stage, commit, or push occurred; shift-operations-workspace, CVF core, F1A, catalog, Index, and the roadmap were not touched. Claude does not self-grant REVIEW_PASS. Status: REPAIRED_PENDING_INDEPENDENT_RE_REVIEW. Next governed move: Codex acts as independent REVIEWER performing re-review of ADR-OW-006, OW-XR1-SPEC-001, and OW-XR1-WO-001 as repaired.

XR1 authorization repair round 2 (2026-07-24, REPAIR_WORKER, Claude, provider-neutral role contract): independent Codex review returned four findings, all repaired without waiver. XR1-R7B INCOMPLETE_FILTER_LIFECYCLE_SEPARATION (renamed from a reused XR1-R7 id in round 3's governance-id cleanup; round 1's closed XR1-R7 is unaffected) -- replaced every scan-side five-way partition/disposition statement with exactly four mutually exclusive scan-time classifications (HARD_EXCLUDE, PROTECTED_SOURCE_ONLY, QUARANTINE_REVIEW, ELIGIBLE_CANDIDATE); restated APPROVED_APPLY as a separate post-review/apply lifecycle state excluded from scan classification counts, never emitted by the scan engine, and never appearing anywhere in a scan report; corrected scan step 6, section C's table/vocabulary/sum, the ADR-OW-001 comparison paragraph, the claim boundary (section F), XR1-AC-21, and the matching stop condition; the sum of the four scan-classification counts now equals the total logical accounting records exactly. XR1-R8 OPERATION_SCHEMA_AND_DESTINATION_DRIFT -- defined the apply-manifest entry schema as an operation-keyed oneOf: new/modified requires candidateSourcePath/candidateSourceBlobSha256/candidateSourceGitMode plus destinationPath and destinationPrecondition; deleted requires baseSourcePath/baseBlobSha256 plus destinationPath and destinationPrecondition and never requires or accepts a nonexistent candidate blob; renamed requires oldSourcePath, newSourcePath, baseBlobSha256, candidateBlobSha256, oldDestinationPath, newDestinationPath, contentChanged, and a destinationPrecondition for each destination. Every destinationPrecondition explicitly states ABSENT or PRESENT plus the expected SHA-256; apply rejects before writing if the current destination state/hash differs and rechecks immediately before mutation. Added positive/negative acceptance criteria for every operation, destination drift, rename collision, and deletion of a locally modified destination. XR1-R9 RECOVERY_ARTIFACTS_OUTSIDE_CEILING -- defined exact recovery output locations and added them to the XR1-O-C2 BUILD/runtime ceiling: raw preimage bytes are local and gitignored under .cvf/local-linked-source-recovery/<run-id>/** and are never staged or committed; tracked-safe metadata receipts (apply_receipt.json, failure_recovery_receipt.json) live under provenance/shift-operations/<candidate>/apply/<manifest-sha>/ and contain only hashes/statuses, never raw preimage bytes, matched secret values, credentials, or absolute local paths; extended the authorized .gitignore modification accordingly and stated retention/cleanup behavior for local recovery bundles. XR1-R10 SCAN_SIDE_EFFECT_CONTRADICTION -- repaired XR1-AC-13 and related statements: scan may create/update only its declared provenance output and the ignored .cvf/local-workspace-link.json; creation of a fresh peer clone may occur only at the bounded, validated clone destination; no other Operations or existing Shift tracked/worktree state may change; added a test requirement using git check-ignore proving the local binding is absent from normal and staged Git status. XR1 authorization repair round 3 (2026-07-24, REPAIR_WORKER, Claude, provider-neutral role contract): independent Codex review returned three findings, all repaired without waiver, plus a governance-id cleanup. XR1-R11 CIRCULAR_COMMIT_BINDING -- withdrew authorizationCommit from the apply-manifest schema (a file cannot contain the hash of the Git commit that contains that same file); ADR-OW-006 section G rewritten to a non-circular two-artifact model: the manifest carries only schemaVersion/workspaceId/base/candidate/policy/dataset fields, per-entry operation fields, authorizationReceiptPath, and manifestSha256; a separate independent review receipt (authored only by Codex REVIEWER) carries receiptSchemaVersion, decision (exactly REVIEW_PASS), manifestPath, manifestSha256, reviewed baseCommit/candidateCommit, scanDatasetSha256/filteringPolicySha256, reviewer role, and a review-evidence reference; apply is invoked with external --authorization-commit/--authorization-receipt CLI arguments, never fields read from inside the manifest. Verification now requires: the given commit reachable from Operations origin/main; the manifest and receipt blobs both existing byte-identically at that commit; the receipt naming this exact manifest hash with decision REVIEW_PASS; the receipt's dataset/policy hashes matching the manifest's; and the working manifest's own hash matching both the committed blob and the receipt. New required tests reject an arbitrary/unpushed local authorization commit, a manifest/receipt blob missing or byte-different at that commit, a receipt naming a different manifest hash, mismatched dataset/policy hashes, and a manifest carrying its own self-declared approval field. XR1-R12 OPERATION_PRECONDITION_TOO_PERMISSIVE -- the renamed operation's destinationPrecondition is split into two exactly-named fields: oldDestinationPrecondition (must be PRESENT with the exact expected SHA-256) and newDestinationPrecondition (must be ABSENT); new/modified/deleted preconditions are likewise constrained exactly by operation (new: ABSENT; modified/deleted: PRESENT+hash). A rename whose newDestinationPath collides with another destination only under case-insensitive or Unicode-normalization comparison remains rejected under the existing collision rule. New tests cover every invalid operation/precondition combination, destination drift, and the immediate pre-mutation recheck. XR1-R13 SECRET_SCAN_SCOPE_CONTRADICTION -- separated XR1-O-C2 BUILD authorization (builds and tests the tool using synthetic temporary Git fixtures only; performs no real scan/apply against Shift content and reads no real secret) from a future real Shift scan, which requires its own bounded execution work order naming the exact candidate commit; during such an authorized future run the detector may stream Git blob bytes locally for classification only, but must never expose them to a human, agent, provider call, log, prompt, or tracked file; HARD_EXCLUDE blobs can never enter an apply manifest; the blanket 'any secret read' exclusion and the 'secret detection' stop condition were both corrected to prohibit human/agent/provider/log/tracked-file exposure rather than prohibiting detection itself (HARD_EXCLUDE classification is expected behavior, not a violation). Governance-id cleanup: round 2's reused XR1-R7 finding is renamed XR1-R7B throughout ADR-OW-006, OW-XR1-SPEC-001, OW-XR1-WO-001, and this continuity file; round 1's original XR1-R7 (FILTER_PRECEDENCE_AND_SECRET_CONTENT) is unchanged and remains closed under its own id. Repaired exactly the six paths this round's ceiling permits (ADR-OW-006, OW-XR1-SPEC-001, OW-XR1-WO-001, IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.json, CVF_SESSION/ACTIVE_SESSION_STATE.json, CVF_SESSION/handoffs/AGENT_HANDOFF_V1_2026-07-23.md); no seventh path; no BUILD, stage, commit, or push occurred; shift-operations-workspace, CVF core, F1A, catalog, Index, and the roadmap were not touched; all three Markdown files remain under 600 lines (ADR-OW-006 596, OW-XR1-SPEC-001 414, OW-XR1-WO-001 359). Claude does not self-grant REVIEW_PASS and did not stage, commit, or push. Status at handoff: REPAIRED_PENDING_INDEPENDENT_RE_REVIEW_3.
